meaning: n.孤儿,失去生母的幼小动物adj.无双亲的,孤儿的vt.使成孤儿
examples: He stayed with a raccoon orphan he had adopted.
It's the latest in a long line of orphan literature.
He brought up the orphan and passed onto him his knowledge of medicine.
Do not miss the documentary Kusasi, From Orphan to King, about the campsite's most famous inhabitant.
In 1924, the comic strip "Little Orphan Annie, " by Harold Gray, made its debut.
